code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, compact sentence.
    """
    # Refined mapping – shorter, more natural phrasing.
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "started construction in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "designed by",
        "owner": "owned by",
        "formerName": "formerly known as",
        "height": "stands",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "developed by",
        "tenant": "has tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is in",
        "currentTenants": "houses",
        "address": "has address",
        "inaugurationDate": "inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in",
        "state": "is in",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "built in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "added to NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "known for the building",
        "governingBody": "governed by",
        "leader": "has leader",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "has postal code",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to build a single clause per subject.
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    sentences = []
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        parts = []
        # Determine if we have a building type – it should start the clause.
        building_type = next((g.object for g in group if g.predicate == "buildingType"), None)
        if building_type:
            parts.append(f"{subject} is a {building_type}")
        # Process remaining predicates.
        for triple in group:
            if triple.predicate == "buildingType":
                continue
            verb = verb_map.get(triple.predicate, "has")
            obj = triple.object
            # Special handling for height to add units if missing.
            if triple.predicate == "height":
                # strip possible "(metres)" text
                obj = obj.replace("(metres)", "").strip()
                parts.append(f"{subject} {verb} {obj} metres high")
            elif triple.predicate in {"floorCount", "bedCount", "numberOfRooms"}:
                parts.append(f"{subject} {verb} {obj} floors")
            else:
                # For location we don't repeat the subject.
                if triple.predicate == "location":
                    parts.append(f"{verb} {obj}")
                else:
                    parts.append(f"{subject} {verb} {obj}")
        # Join parts with commas, add 'and' before last if more than one.
        if len(parts) == 1:
            clause = parts[0]
        else:
            clause = ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1]
        sentences.append(clause)

    # Combine subject clauses into final sentence.
    if not sentences:
        return ""
    if len(sentences) == 1:
        final = sentences[0] + "."
    else:
        final = "; ".join(sentences) + "."
    return final[0].upper() + final[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
